title: acetosella peduncularis
intro: Acetosella peduncularis, also known as the mountain cranberry or rock cranberry, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Acetosella peduncularis include its ability to help regulate blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a raw fruit or used in jams and preserves.
